answers.p4q2: RQ1: "When/how do knowledge workers perceive critical thinking enaction with GenAI?" RQ2: "When/why does GenAI change critical thinking effort?" RQ1 focuses occurrence/methods, RQ2 examines effort dynamics.
answers.p4q4: For Analysis/Synthesis/Evaluation, effort shifts from content creation to oversight/integration of AI outputs. Creates ztewardship role where workers guide AI toward quality standards they remain accountable for.
answers.p4q8: Key barriers: insufficient domain expertise, inability to improve AI responses, time pressure. Largest participant reports were difficulties inspecting/verifying AI outputs (hardest to overcome).
answers.p4q10: Stewardship better captures humans directing AI output (not equal collaboration/supervision). Emphasizes human retains full accountability AI executes, human judges final responsibility.
answers.p4q6: Motivational factors (quality desire, error avoidance) spark critical thinking intent, but ability barriers (knowledge gaps, AI stubbornness) block execution. Breakdowns happen most when workers want accuracy but lack skills/time to verify outputs.

answers.p6q7: Mediation worked for workload/time pressure → all outcomes (procrastination, memory loss, poor CGPA). Reward sensitivity mediated only procrastination/memory loss (not CGPA). Quality sensitivity showed no mediation effects.
answers.p6q2: H3a predicted reward sensitivity increases ChatGPT use (risk-taking view); H3b predicted decrease (cheating fear). H3b supported grade-worried students avoid AI to prevent academic penalties.
answers.p6q10: Excessive ChatGPT reliance cuts cognitive engagement needed for memory consolidation. Authors argue students skip active learning/recall practice, leading to weaker retention over time.
answers.p6q4: Began with 12 items → 5 experts validated content, kept 10 → EFA revealed items 4&9 had poor loadings/communalities , dropped them. Final 8 item scale had strong reliability and one factor structure.
answers.p6q9: Since workload and time pressure spike ChatGPT use, authors recommend teachers lighten assignments and deadlines. Also guide students to treat ChatGPT as a learning aid, not a full replacement for their own work.

answers.p5q7: 1. increased AI advice acceptance,2. improved trust calibration (follow correct/reject wrong). Qualitative review found stronger evidence for mechanism 1, explanations mainly boost acceptance.
answers.p5q10: explanations may increase blind AI acceptance rather than critical evaluation. Triggered by qualitative finding that XAI primarily boosts acceptance, not calibrated reliance.
answers.p5q4: Tabular data: null XAI effect, Text data: strong positive, Authors explain text's intuitive nature aids human judgment of explanations vs. tabular's complexity.
answers.p5q9: Fugener et al. (confidence ratings ↓ AI adherence), Bansal et al./van der Waa et al. (explanations ↑ acceptance even for incorrect predictions). Shows unresolved tension in literature.
answers.p5q2: Primary comparisons: XAI vs human only (XAI superior, XAI vs AI only (no difference). Second finding tempers first you can't attribute XAI gains specifically to explanations.

responses.p6comments: Paper was a bit more digestable due to highlights

finalResponses.fq_like_most_least: highlights probably helped overall but in a time crunched scenario is contributed to the noise
finalResponses.fq_missing_confusing_unnecessary: final questionnaire MC felt easy to accomplish and answer selection seemed logical and not necessarily paper dependent

answers.cpq2: Critical thinking paper shows effort shifts from Analysis/Synthesis/Evaluation execution to verification/integration of AI outputs. AI automates production, forcing humans into oversight role
answers.cpq4: University paper: workload pressure → ChatGPT shortcuts → procrastination  + memory loss. Reduced cognitive effort skips active learning needed for retention/performance.
answers.cpq6: Self confidence vs AI confidence. High self confidence predicts more verification/evaluation effort; high AI confidence predicts less critical checking.
answers.cpq8: Time pressure creates shortcut mentality university paper found time pressure positively predicts ChatGPT use
answers.cpq10: Improves performance: XAI boosts accuracy vs humans , ChatGPT aids stressed students. Creates risks: reduces verification effort , increases blind acceptance

crossFinalAnswers.cpq10: Improves performance: XAI boosts accuracy vs humans , ChatGPT aids stressed students. Creates risks: reduces verification effort , increases blind acceptance
